_comment: Robot Builder skill — YouTube intro. Narration pre-generated with ElevenLabs (audio/vo1-7.mp3), so stage 1 (narrate.py) is skipped.

scenes.narration: Meet Robot Builder — a skill that turns Claude into your personal robotics mentor.
From your very first parts list to fully autonomous machines — it guides ground rovers, boats and submersibles, and aircraft, matched to your budget and your skill level.

title.head: Ground  •  Water  •  Air
title.sub: Rovers   •   boats & ROVs   •   drones, planes & rockets

scenes.narration: A nine-rung build ladder takes you from flashing an SD card, through sensors, ROS, and control theory, all the way to machine learning — one safe, tested step at a time.

title.head: The 9-rung build ladder
title.sub: parts → SD card → sensors → ROS → control → AI

scenes.narration: To prove it, the skill designed this virtual self-balancing robot in a physics simulator — then tuned a classic cascaded PID controller that shrugs off a shove.

title.head: Cascaded PID
title.sub: MuJoCo physics  •  tuned like the real thing

scenes.narration: Then it wrapped the same robot in a training gym, and taught a neural network to balance from scratch — three hundred thousand steps, about five minutes on an ordinary laptop.
And everything ships with hard rules: battery discipline, legal flight, secure connections — and A.I. never gets direct control of the motors.
Clone it. Install it. Start building. Robot Builder — from first motor to machine learning.
